# terrible_splunk_es_splunk_adaptive_response_notable_event (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `correlation_search_name` (String) Name of correlation search to associate this notable event adaptive response with
- `description` (String) Description of the notable event, this will populate the description field for the web console
- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) Name of notable event
- `state` (String) Add or remove a data source.

### Optional

- `asset_extraction` (String) list of assets to extract, select any one or many of the available choices defaults to all available choices
- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `default_owner` (String) Default owner of the notable event, if unset it will default to Splunk System Defaults
- `default_status` (String) Default status of the notable event, if unset it will default to Splunk System Defaults
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `drill_down_earliest_offset` (String) Set the amount of time before the triggering event to search for related events. For example, 2h. Use \"$info_min_time$\" to set the drill-down time to match the earliest time of the search
- `drill_down_latest_offset` (String) Set the amount of time after the triggering event to search for related events. For example, 1m. Use \"$info_max_time$\" to set the drill-down time to match the latest time of the search
- `drill_down_name` (String) Name for drill down search, Supports variable substitution with fields from the matching event.
- `drill_down_search` (String) Drill down search, Supports variable substitution with fields from the matching event.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `identity_extraction` (String) list of identity fields to extract, select any one or many of the available choices defaults to all available choices
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `investigation_profiles` (String) Investigation profile to assiciate the notable event with.
- `next_steps` (String) List of adaptive responses that should be run next Describe next steps and response actions that an analyst could take to address this threat.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `recommended_actions` (String) List of adaptive responses that are recommended to be run next Identifying Recommended Adaptive Responses will highlight those actions for the analyst when looking at the list of response actions available, making it easier to find them among the longer list of available actions.
- `security_domain` (String) Splunk Security Domain
- `severity` (String) Severity rating
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
